messages.content:
Dr. Nathan Thomas's home in Michigan was one of dozens of stops on the underground railroad (the
network of people and places that some enslaved people used to escape to freedom). The word
underground may lead one to assume that Thomas Kept his antislavery views a secret. _______ his
views were quite well known.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
a) A. Thus,
c) C. In reality,
b) B. Similarly,
d) D. For instance,

Space scientist Stefanie Milam is excited about the potential of the new James Webb Space
Telescope (JWST). Unlike its predecessor, the Hubble Telescope, the JWST is primarily an infrared
telescope. Most light from distant galaxies is infrared; _______ the IWST can provide Milam and her
team much more information about these galaxies than the Hubble could.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
a) A. consequently,
c) C. additionally,
b) B. in contrast,
d) D. actually.

Newer offshore wind turbines often outperform older models. For example, Denmark’s offshore wind
farm ,built in 1995, has wind turbines that generate about 1 megawatt of electricity each. ______the
Netherlands’ offshore wind farms, built in 2021 , has turbines that can generate about 10 megawatts
of electricity each.
Which choice completes the text with most logical transition?
a) A. Granted,
c) C. For this reason,
b) B. Eventually,
d) D. In contrast,

The composite volcano Actan in Guatemala has steep sides and a cone shape ._______ the cinder
cone volcano Tantalus in Hawaii also features steep sides and a cone shape
Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
a) A. For example,
c) C. By contrast,
b) B. Thus,
d) D. Similarly,

While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
- Togo is a country in west Africa
- A high percentage of Togo’s population (41 percent is under fifteen years old.
- It has the twenty- seventh under fifteen population in the world.
- Roughly 40 percent of Africa's population is under fifteen years old- the highest of any continent.
- According to the United Nations (UN), Africa's “high number of young people is an opportunity for
the continent’s growth- but only if these new generations are fully empowered to realize their best
potential “
The student wants to emphasize the global rank of Togo's youth population. Which choice
most effectively uses information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
a) A. "Only if these new generations are fully
empowered to realize their best potential,"
says the UN, will Africa's high percentage of
young people lead to the continent’s growth.
c) C. Africa's high population of young people is
due in part to the high percentage of young
people in Togo.
b) B. Making up roughly 40 percent of the
continent's total population, Africa's under
fteen population offers “an opportunity for the
continent's growth," according to the UN.
d) D. With 41 percent of its population under
fifteen years of age, Togo has the twenty
seventh largest population for that age range
in the world.
d) D. With 41 percent of its population under fifteen years of age, Togo has the twenty-seventh largest population for that age range in the world.

While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
- Thomas Edison is regarded as one of the most important inventors in US history.
- Having received little formal schooling, Edison relied on specialist employees at his various
laboratories and officers to complete the technical work for many of his inventions.
- Frank . J sprague was a naval officer who worked at the Menio Park laboratory.
- He is best known for being the father of electric traction · George F. Morrison was a business
executive who worked at Edison lamp works.
- He is best known for pioneering the production of the incandescent lamp.
The student wants to emphasize that the two employees were known for different
contributions to the field of technology. Which choice most effectively uses information from
the given sentences to accomplish this goal?
a) A. Among Thomas Edisons employees was
Frank J. Sprague, best known for being the
father of electric traction.
c) C. Beyond working for Thomas Edison, Frank
J. Sprague and George F. Morrison were
known for distinct career achievements:
Sprague for being the father of electric traction
and Morrison for pioneering the production of
the incandescent lamp.
b) B. Frank J. Sprague, a naval ocer, and
George F. Morrison, a business executive,
both worked for Thomas Edison.
d) D. Thomas Edisons’ historic success would
not have been possible without business
executive George F. Morrison.

c) C. Beyond working for Thomas Edison, Frank J. Sprague and George F. Morrison were known for distinct career achievements: Sprague for being the father of electric traction and Morrison for pioneering the production of the incandescent lamp.

By placing sites such as Canada’s Nahanni National Park on its World Heritage List, the United
Nations Educational, Scientific, and Cultural Organization champions their preservation, in the hope
that they will not be wilfully damaged or left to __________
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A. provoke.
c) C. deteriorate.
b) B. regenerate.
d) D. diversify.

Posed in 1970, McMullen's g-conjecture ________ many mathematicians before yielding to the
efforts of Karim Adiprasito, who presented a proof of it in 2018.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A. appeased
c) C. disregarded
b) B. indoctrinated
d) D. confounded

In September of 1989, Indonesia liberalized its stock market, meaning that it began allowing foreign
individuals and businesses to invest money in Indonesian companies. This was part of a wave of
stock market liberalizations from the mid-1980s through the mid-1990s Venezuela in 1990,
Bangladesh in 1991, and so on. In an analysis of economic data from 1976 to 1993, Ross Levine
and Sara Zeros found that liberalization did not lead to enduring increases in investment in
companies based in countries that liberalized. Peter Blair Henry, however, found that, on average,
investment in companies in liberalized countries increased significantly in the three years following
liberalization. Taken together, these results suggest that ________
Which choice most logically completes the text?
a) A. it typically takes at least three years for
companies to benefit from government
policies allowing foreign investment, but
governments rarely maintain such policies for
that long
c) C. investment growth is likely to be more
consistent in countries that liberalize than in
countries that do not.
b) B. companies based in countries that begin
allowing foreign investment will probably see
short-term increases in investment, but that
these gains are unlikely to last.
d) D. economists' expectations about the effect
of liberalization on investment were largely
correct.
b) B. companies based in countries that begin allowing foreign investment will probably see short-term increases in investment, but that these gains are unlikely to last.
